title: Python planning runtime facade slice

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Advance #892 by routing generated planning Python operation-executor source-runtime imports through generated-local facade modules, making remaining planning runtime delegates explicit and mechanically auditable.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Do not claim full #892 completion. Do not hand-edit generated planning outputs except by regeneration. Keep behavior identical; this slice isolates the boundary and adds proof against direct planning source-runtime imports in the generated operation executor.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Exact facade module names, generator factoring, static proof wording, and narrow validation commands.
canonical_core.escalate_when: A better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.
canonical_core.next_action: Add planning local runtime bindings, teach generated function-call handlers to use local facades, regenerate outputs, and prove direct planning source-runtime imports are absent from generated/planning/python/primitives/operation_executor.py.
completion_criteria: Generated planning operation executor imports generated-local planning facade modules instead of repo_planning_bootstrap.installer or repo_planning_bootstrap.runtime_projection directly.
Generated facade modules are produced from command_package_ir local_runtime_bindings and expose every remaining planning installer/runtime_projection delegate used by operation executor handlers.
Static proof rejects regression to direct planning source-runtime imports in generated/planning/python/primitives/operation_executor.py.
The slice is archived as bounded progress with #892 still open because facade delegates remain transitional runtime debt.
